q:
relative pronoun
 An architect is someone ___ designs buildings.
a: An architect is someone who designs buildings.

q:
relative pronoun
 A customer is someone ___ buys something from a shop.
a: A customer is someone who buys something from a shop.

q:
relative pronoun
 A burglar is someone ___ breaks into a house to steal things.
a: A burglar is someone who breaks into a house to steal things.

q:
relative pronoun
 A shoplifter is someone ___ steals from a shop.
a: A shoplifter is someone who steals from a shop.

q:
relative pronoun
 A tenant is someone ___ pays rent to live somewhere.
a: A tenant is someone who pays rent to live somewhere.

q:
relative pronoun
 A liar is someone ___ doesn't tell the truth.
a: A liar is someone who doesn't tell the truth.

q:
relative pronoun
 A coward is someone ___ is not brave.
a: A coward is someone who is not brave.

q:
relative pronoun
 A pessimist is someone ___ expects the worst to happen.
a: A pessimist is someone who expects the worst to happen.

q:
combine sentences with a relative pronoun
 A girl was injured in the accident. She is now in hospital.
 The girl ___ was injured in the accident is now in hospital.
a: The girl who was injured in the accident is now in hospital.

q:
combine sentences with a relative pronoun
 A waiter served us. He was impolite and impatient.
 The waiter ___ served us was impolite and impatient.
a: The waiter who served us was impolite and impatient.

q:
combine sentences with a relative pronoun
 A building was destroyed in the fire. It has now been rebuilt.
 The building ___ was destroyed in the fire has now been rebuilt.
a: The building which was destroyed in the fire has now been rebuilt.

q:
combine sentences with a relative pronoun
 Some people were arrested. They have now been released.
 The people ___ were arrested have now been released.
a: The people who were arrested have now been released.

q:
combine sentences with a relative pronoun
 A bus goes to the airport. It runs every half hour.
 The bus ___ goes to the airport runs every half hour.
a: The bus which goes to the airport runs every half hour.

q:
relative pronoun
 Helen works for a company ___ makes furniture.
a: Helen works for a company that makes furniture.

q:
relative pronoun
 The movie is about a girl ___ runs away from home.
a: The movie is about a girl who runs away from home.

q:
relative pronoun
 What happened to the pictures ___ were hanging on the wall?
a: What happened to the pictures that were hanging on the wall?

q:
relative pronoun
 A mystery is something ___ cannot be explained.
a: A mystery is something that cannot be explained.

q:
relative pronoun
 I've heard it's a good hotel, but I don't know anyone ___ has stayed there.
a: I've heard it's a good hotel, but I don't know anyone who has stayed there.

q:
relative pronoun
 History is the study of things ___ happened in the past.
a: History is the study of things that happened in the past.

q:
relative pronoun
 Albert Einstein was the scientist ___ developed the theory of relativity.
a: Albert Einstein was the scientist who developed the theory of relativity.

q:
relative pronoun
 It seems that Earth is the only planet ___ can support life.
a: It seems that Earth is the only planet that can support life.

q:
correct the relative pronoun
 I don't like stories 'who' have unhappy endings.
a: I don't like stories that have unhappy endings.

q:
correct the relative pronoun
 Where's the nearest shop 'who' sells bread?
a: Where's the nearest shop that sells bread?

q:
correct the relative pronoun
 Dan said some things about me 'they' were not true.
a: Dan said some things about me that were not true.

q:
correct the relative pronoun
 The driver 'which' caused the accident was fined £500.
a: The driver who caused the accident was fined £500.

q:
correct the relative pronoun
 We live in a world 'what' is changing all the time.
a: We live in a world that is changing all the time.

q:
correct the relative pronoun
 What was the name of the horse 'what' won the race?
a: What was the name of the horse that won the race?
